author: Human
text: How does agent memory retrieval work in this repo?

author: Claude
text: Agent memory retrieval in this repo uses the AMS (Associative Memory Store) system. The core workflow is: search for relevant sessions using semantic queries via `./scripts/ams search`, then drill into specific sessions with `./scripts/ams read <id>`. The Rust kernel handles indexing and ranking. On Linux, use ./scripts/ams; on Windows, use scripts\ams.bat.

author: Claude
text: The swarm-plan system is a tree-structured task tracking system for multi-agent workflows. It supports push/pop/observe operations to record work hierarchically. Plans are stored as per-plan JSONL files in shared-memory/system-memory/swarm-plans/. Use `./scripts/ams swarm-plan context` to see the active plan.

author: Human
text: How do I bootstrap AMS on Linux?

author: Claude
text: To bootstrap AMS on Linux: run ./scripts/sync-all-agent-memory.sh --no-browser. This ingests sessions from ~/.claude/projects and ~/.codex/sessions. In a fresh container without those directories, use --claude-root or --codex-root to point at your session directories, or --raw to provide a pre-built corpus file directly.
